reviews.review_description: Loving the game but I absolutely hate the sword. No training or practice on how to use it, or how to get rid of it to get back to the gun. I can't get the hang of how close the fireballs need to be before I can hit them with the sword effectively with aim. The big orange fireballs just stop next to me and no idea what that means, did I hit them or not? Is it a glitch? So frustrating

My son and I both found this frustrating and quickly overwhelming. You get shot before you can do much, and you have to keep re-making your wand. It got repetitive quickly, and son lost interest after 10 minutes

Visual: 4/5
- cheerful but lacking detail
- enemies look boring except bosses
- not much level variation

Audio/voice/story: 5/5
- fun intro comics
- good music and sound effects

Gameplay: 3/5
- weapon loading & usage is finicky
- boring enemy behavior (except bosses?)
- sometimes confusing
- some seemingly pointless mechanics (e.g. "sponching")
Bought yesterday, played for 5 minuets and found it wasnt for me and i cant request a refund.

developer_response.body: Hello litedlepr. We're sorry to hear that the game doesn't fit your taste - and in any case, when this happen so quickly you should be entitled to return the game. May I suggest you contact Oculus support? They are the ones able to troubleshoot purchases and refunds issues. Best.

reviews.review_description:
Update:

You definitely need some time to get to know the gameplay, and it still can be quite frustrating at times but once you get the hang of it, it can get  fun with all the extra updates on the spells and weapons you start to buy (although you kinda have to figure out yourself how the weapon /spell works in the battle itself, which is kinda weird) Definitely hope to see more updates and improvements in the near future.... Can only imagine it to get better with a stronger core. 1* extra! 

I had my  eye on this game, so with the discount I finally made the step .... Wasn't sure what to expect.... Yet another rogue - like game with limited progression. Already had a few games like these...."Until you fall" and "In death :Unchained" were good and fun.
The game starts of good, it's not easy, and you really gotta learn how to handle the spells. But ok, after some rounds of concurring some planets, dying, starting over again, and again, and again.... I finally get to beat the first loooong boss fight. Excited to move on to the second system. And I'm happy with the latest update that saves part of your progress, cause otherwise I already would have deleted it from my device. 
But.... From the second universe on the difficulty level spikes immediately.  The first system really doesn't prepare you for what's to come....frustration and repetitive gameplay. Ducking left, right, down...... It's almost like being an acrobat  to dodge the firing shots. 
So for now, not sure if I really like the game. Will continue exploring and maybe after getting some upgrades on the spells  it might get more fun.
Definitely some points on the colorful worlds and the original approach..... But way to hard.
Almost feels like a punishment for trying to have some fun in a game.

developer_response.body: Hello TVL1977. Thanks for taking the plunge and buying the game! We have made lots of adjustments to the game's difficulty in the past updates and are always working on getting players a more streamlined progression. Part of it is indeed in unlocking better spells and perks when you die, and you should feel you're getting better pretty quickly - and have much more fun doing so. Best.

reviews.review_title: Great Graphics and a good work out
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoy using this game for a workout the dodging and ducking keeps me on the move.  The graphics are terrific.  I was a little frustrated at first because the fights seemed way to hard and no lives were restored between planets. Since the upgrade, I love it.  Shouting was OK but I love the sword.

I would like to see a weapons training area that instructs you how to use each weapon and gives you a place to practice.

developer_response.body:
Hello junger2000!
Thank you for your review, great to read you're enjoying the update! 
Noted regarding the dojo, we have a couple of ideas in mind. 
See you on Lunar Groves!

reviews.review_title: Definitely a unique game in its niche
reviews.review_description:
So when I say unique I mean it. This game definitely stand out as one that is different in an incredible way. The visuals are spectacular and the music is highly original. I'll admit I spent the first ten minutes trying to wrap my head around everything that was going on in the intro, but once I got my bearing I had a ton of fun with it. 

It's got incredible potential as further development continues on it to become something that truly stands out in the niche and guides things for the better. The devs are very responsive on Discord and always looking for feedback on how to improve the game, something you don't always see. It's a shame this is an unknown gem on the Oculus Store as it's one that I truly think should be highlighted more often. Do yourself a favor and check this game out. You won't regret it.

developer_response.body:
Thanks Vandlan, what a great review!
The early days of a new platform are always an exciting moment to try new things and get a grip on the medium, and it genuinely is fantastic to read you're enjoying our work! Thanks also for giving feedback on Discord and being so involved with us. 
Cheers from Paris,
Yann

reviews.review_description:
Glitches a lot , 
i have huge framedrops on the second weapon (playtime about 10 minutes untill i get it , so i cant progress)
Very very pixelated graphics compared to PC version which is still not on Oculus Store (for Rift) let alone crossbuy .....
very dissapointed .

I really tried to like it but since the very beggining the experience I had with this game was absolutely dissapointing , comparing it with the rest of the Quest games

I really cannot understand as a dev myself whats so performance heavy in this game..... really .....

developer_response.body:
Happy new year, NEETShonen! Thanks for your review.
We have looked into the performance issues and will be pushing a fix soon.
Pixelated vs a PC is unfortunately quite normal... The Quest isn't the most powerful of headsets. But we've crafted the art so it looks great on Quest, as well - and we've just pushed further upgrades, and we've got great reviews. And we've also slashed loading times by a further 40%. Would love to hear what you think of our latest work!

reviews.review_description:
I love roguelites, so I had to give this game a try. Sadly, was disappointed.
 
- controls are clunky and gimmicky, get in the way.
- enemy design is completely generic, you are shooting at basic geometric shapes, literally.
- loading screens feel like they comprise 50% of game time. Want to upgrade a weapon? Loading screen. Finished a level after 1 minute? Loading screen. New level? Loading screen...

Overall not fun at all, but has very nice looking graphics. Wasted potential.

developer_response.body:
Hello Ovi_S, thanks for playing Star Shaman!

We are pushing an update with a lot of new spells very (very) soon, which gives the Shaman Sphere a lot more depth. The feeling when you've got 4 attack spells on your dominant hand and three defensive ones on the other is incredibly satisfying: like you have tactical options at your fingertips - literally. We'd love to hear what you think about the casting systems after the update. We also heard the community on the enemy designs and have really cool updated ones - more in the Transformers vibe -  coming soon. As for the loading/teleportation tunnels, we're afraid this has more to do with the Quest's limitations than with anything else. However, we have good news as we've been able to squeeze the loading times down to a mere 4 seconds between levels, while pushing resolution and graphic upgrades on Quest 1 and 2.

We are committed to making Star Shaman a deeply satisfying game. Give us another go after our next update and let us know what you think, either here, or on our Discord! Looking forward to your thoughts!

Yann

reviews.review_description: I like the game and the music, just wish there was a purchasable checkpoint, or a bigger reward to beating a boss.

developer_response.body:
Hello SuperSainSam! 

Great to hear you're enjoying the game, and the music. It's already available on bandcamp, and shortly on all music streaming platforms (Spotify, Apple Music, Deezer, ...)

We're working on a new arcade-style game mode, but in the meantime, we've just released a major update with new spells, a revised progression, graphic updates and 90 Hz on Quest. Be sure to play it and let us know what you think! We're on Discord: https://discord.gg/9g7vA7J

See you out there, Star Shaman.

reviews.review_title: I really wish I liked this
reviews.review_description:
I wanted to like this so much more. I mean, I DO like that you are shooting, and things are shooting back at you. But the gun runs out of juice, and all of the sudden you need to re-arm yourself. This should be a good thing, but for me, I can't get the controls to work well enough to do this the first time I try. So, I'm being shot at and killed before I can re-arm. It's frustrating because it bounces back, and you are left without a gun. (And every time you come back to life, you get thrown into a vortex that I have to close my eyes for so I don't puke)

What kept me going were the graphics. When I play in VR the experience is what makes it gratifying. When I "saved" the first world I was, like, WHOA! Look at all the cool creatures! I could not wait to save another planet to see what was there in the next world. But, oddly enough, when I saved that world, it looked exactly the same. All the creatures flying around were the same images (and there are Chinese-style architecture ruins in the background on every planet, for some reason)

So I tried one more time, finally saved the planet, and got the same result. Three planets looked exactly the same when I saved them.  I am wondering.... since I die so many times, does the system have me always saving the same planet, even through I choose different ones? or is there always going to be the same "win" screen?

This is a big problem for me, because I want to get to that next big visual. But there is no payoff for me if I am always seeing the same thing.

At this point I am really considering getting a refund. I can't see myself jumping in to play this for fun, when I am so frustrated and not getting any payoff.

Am I missing something?

developer_response.body:
Hello LordoftheRing! Very cool to hear you're enjoying our universe! 

Here's how we did it. There are 3 solar systems, each with their own theme. The first one is Asia, the second the Nordics, and the third one North America. 

Per solar-system, there are 4 types of planets:
- the big, central, Cradle planet
- the "standard difficulty" planet
- the "elite difficulty" planet
- the "interstellar" planet
Each has its own 3D asset, animals, and skybox.

We would have LOVED to squeeze more variety and randomness into the game, but we are severely limited by the Quest's memory and processing power.

So instead of having mediocre, but more varied art, we decided to reduce variety just a little bit to give us room to push our art to the absolute maximum - the result of which we are very happy to hear you are enjoying. 

We will be adding more moons and more skyboxes to each planet in an update coming soon, in order to push variety further. More solar-systems may come in the future, too. 

As for the Shaman Sphere, just make sure you grab the crystal full hand and it should work like a charm! Have you tried the Astral Sword yet? It's incredibly satisfying. And we've got more spells coming soon, too.

Keep saving those planets, and we promise when standalone VR becomes more powerful, we'll be the first ones to push the limits!

reviews.review_description:
First off, I really love the art style and soundtrack! This game has a really unique play style which I like. The rogue-lite element is fun. There are however some problems that make the game less enjoyable than it could be.

- entering the shop takes forever
- spells on the sphere are close together and easy to misgrab
- astral life display is almost never showing, could you put this permanently visible in/on the sphere instead?
- shield feels a bit useless so my non-dominant hand is barely doing anything. How about using any weapon with any hand?
- boss fight one is a bit meh, I love the enemies elsewhere but this guy is a tad generic.

developer_response.body:
[UPDATE JAN 2, 2021] Hello ScrimBot, and happy new year! We've just updated the game, and would love your thoughts! We've got new spells, a new progression with permanent upgrades, an awesome new HUD, we've made the shop snappier, pushed graphics up, and improved quality of life in every corner. And since you love the soundtrack, I'm happy to say you can listen to it on Spotify! https://open.spotify.com/album/2jz7QSrm07iAh4BJJUubU9
My favorite is Lunar Groves, it was actually made on my request ;)

Hello ScrimBot, thank you for your rating, and your comments. Great to hear you are enjoying playing with the Sphere. It's unique to Star Shaman and we are quite proud of having designed a spellcasting system that is so widely enjoyed by our players.

I'm happy to report that with the latest update, the astral life is now displayed permanently. The shield is actually quite useful because every projectile you parry is transferred to your current weapon. It's an efficient way of loading up on ammo while in combat! As for your suggestion of using any weapon with any hand, we thought about it and prototyped it, but it would limit the number of weapons you could have at any given time - and since there are more coming... ^^ 

See you on the Milky Way!

reviews.review_description: Awesome game !!

Tried the game for a few minutes. Gameplay is fun, the music is upbeat and gets you in the groove, and the graphics are colourful.
Unfortunately, the UI is rather terrible. The mechanics of the game get lost in all the talking, and there's a lot to read, with text windows often disappearing too fast to be able to read it all.
The game localisation is also terrible: if you set the game to English, you get a bit of French everywhere (the credits are in French, and French strings pop up here and there), and there are terrible misspellings ("Enemies" not "Enemys"). If you set the game to French you get English here and there. I wonder what happens with other languages. They also get English and French thrown in there?
Anyway, not bad, but QA is meant to happen before release, not after!

developer_response.body:
[UPDATE JAN 2, 2021] Bonjour TomBomb_FR! Bonne année! Have you tried our latest update? We've improved the HUD with a really cool Star Shaman face that reacts to damage, and there's a lot less talking and a lot more action in the tutorial. Plus new spells, improved graphics, and a lot of quality of life improvements. Let us know what you think!  

[UPDATE NOV 7, 2020] Hello TomBomb_FR. We have just updated Star Shaman! Come take a look and tell us what you think!

Thanks for your review, and for playing Star Shaman!

We designed the tutorial to introduce players to the unique mechanics as well as to the lore of the game. Once you're through it, the game is more action, less talking. We promise!

Very cool of you to check out the credits! We're a French studio so we've kept them in French. You'll also notice it says "Une production IKIMASHO" under the splash screen.

And you're right about "Enemys" - we've missed that one, and we're fixing it in the next update coming very soon, along with quality of life improvements and other fixes. And there's more coming in the near future to Star Shaman, as well. 

Great to hear you're enjoying yourself outside of these issues! See you in the Nebulae, Star Shaman!

reviews.review_title: Fun game with a big problem...
reviews.review_description:
I know the game just came out and there will be patches and updates in the future. The other reviews are accurate--the game is fun, the art is amazing and so is the music! I won't nitpick every little flaw, but one in particular is a major problem for me and my family...

Why does this game not save your progress as you go? Or at least give you an option to save manually? It's 2020 and we're not playing on the Atari 2600,so you shouldn't have to start the whole game over if you die. Yes, it transfers 10% of your earnings to your next try, but it takes away ALL of your progress.

 Once my friends and I realized that we had to start over after being killed by the first boss, we quickly lost interest in trying to progress through the game any further.

Again, I know the game just released, but until this problem is fixed, I can't really recommend this to any of my friends. Especially for $20.

If this problem can be fixed, I'll definitely give this a solid 5 stars!

developer_response.body:
[UPDATE JAN 2, 2021] - First of all, WE ARE ON SPOTIFY! https://open.spotify.com/album/2jz7QSrm07iAh4BJJUubU9
In our latest update, we've revised the progression: you now unlock weapons permanently, which significantly smoothes the progression and makes you feel ever more powerful. It's really cool to be slashing away at Obeloids with the Astral Sword after a couple of runs.
And as this update testifies, we're working hard on making Star Shaman ever better. So stay tuned for more to come! All the best for 2021!

Thanks for your review, and for playing Star Shaman! It's great to hear you're enjoying the mood of the universe. We've had tremendous fun creating it.
There is an element of roguelite design in the game (like Until you Fall or In Death: Unchained), which indeed means that when you die, you start from the beginning. However, the points you score add up and unlock tiers of more powerful weapons in the Kybele Reliquary, as well as permanent upgrades (more damage, more currency...). As you noted, you also transfer 10% of that score into currency for your next run, which gives you a head start.
And of course, if you want to save your progress during a run, you can do so on the map, at the bottom right with the "Save and Quit" button.
We hope you'll keep enjoying saving the universe!

reviews.review_title: Having fun, although I'm terrible at it!
reviews.review_description: Having an absolute blast. I get what the team means when they say, easy to learn, hard to master. I think it will take me a little bit before I beat this game, but the graphics are beautiful and the music is super fun.  I like the narrative of the game, although I could see some people thinking there is too much reading ( but that is what the continue button is for right?).

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! Super happy to read you're loving it!

reviews.review_description:
The entire game has a wacky, colorful, abstract, sci/fi feelings. It's very fresh, as an artist I really appreciated the amount of work put into this experience. Yes there are a couple bugs here and there, but they are minor and I wouldn't hold it against them on launch day, and none of them affected my experience  The game play is hard to pin down, actually the entire game is hard to pin down, but that's what I find so interesting. If you want something straight forward, this isn't it. 

The world feels very developed art wise, and out of this world. It has a touch of RPG just in the design of characters and flow. The colors remind me of childhood, but on ACID and traveling through space and time.  Thank you for taking chances and making something special. It's projects like this that make VR a special space. For the price I'm not expecting a long game, but looks like there is good replay-ability built in. MORE WEIRD GAMES PLZ!

developer_response.body:
Merci beaucoup, BEAUCOUP! 

It's been the most exhilarating production we've worked on. In 18 months, we've built a new studio, a new team, and a new IP for a platform that had just come out. We're taking chances, trying to find new fun in VR and it's heartwarming to read reviews like yours. It keeps us going.

There are bugs in the game, each one of them hurts when we see it, and we're working hard on fixing them. We want Star Shaman to be a game you feel great in, and love going back to. So it needs to be as polished as humanly possible. There'll also be many improvements and new content coming in the near future.

Thanks again, Star Shaman!

reviews.review_title: A fun game and a light workout
reviews.review_description:
I've played the game for over an hour and after I took off my quest 1 sweat started to build up and that's from normal difficulty! This game really keeps you moving.  I wasn't expecting that this game has a rouge like element to it after dying so no run will be the same. the music is fun, funky and matches the game's unique esthetic. 


there are a few bugs that were mention in the other reviews the crystal stays in your hand after you pull it from the sphere. I did experience the menu sticking to my hand when I purchased the crystal sword(already my personal fav) other than that I haven't experienced any other bugs. I do wish you can keep the spells you acquire from your last run. I did find it a tad annoying that I had to reacquire the free shield every run and since the store very RNG it takes some work to earn enough buy a spell or upgrade. So having a ability to save an additional spell would make a difference especially during boss fights. Also the health bar needs to be more visible to the player. It's too small and out the view of scope I don't realized how much life I have left during fighting waves of enemies so if it was move somewhere the player can easily see it and it was more pronounce that way the player can focus more on dodging and block attacks. otherwise I'm having a blast playing it and would recommend it to anyone looking for a fun upbeat game although, I highly recommend you have a towel next to after your done playing lol.

developer_response.body:
[UPDATE NOW 7, 2020] Hello tigertrack. Good news, we've fixed the annoying "weapon description page sticks in your hand" bug. And following your advice, you now start each run with the shield in your non-dominant hand. Thanks for that! And the health bar is now visible at all times. Your wishes are our command ^^
Looking forward to your thoughts on the updates. Join us on our Discord!

We hear you, tigertrack! We've played the game with the AC all throughout the heatwaves this summer (lucky us, having AC is quite unusual in downtown Paris). 

And while I should be speaking for the whole studio here, the Astral Sword is my favorite, too. The feeling, the effects, the whooshing sound, the haptics. So satisfying, isn't it? We've got more cool(er) stuff coming soon, too.

Upbeat and empowering is what we're going for, and we're super glad to read it's working! See you on Lunar Groves!

reviews.review_description: There should be a bestiary or enemy somewhere in the game, as I have absolutely no idea what most enemies do, wherein I get killed by then immediately because their projectiles do something that I can't seem to dodge. I love the aesthetics and the combat feels fun, but there is a SEVERE lack of information ANYWHERE.

reviews.review_title: Love the style, hate the Rogue
reviews.review_description:
This could be so good. The graphics, style and music are great! I am not generally a fan of rogue likes, and while I've had a couple of recent counter examples. I either suck, or I missed something important in the intro, or this game needs difficulty settings. I played enough to see how I could get better, but losing all my progress each time was just too frustrating. I'm done and it's uninstalled.

A couple suggestions for future projects. It needs a better tutorial. I didn't even know where my health was for the first 30 minutes. It needs difficulty settings. In the early level (I don't know if the game changes later), the rogue like elements are meaningless. Just moving the planets around doesn't improve replayability without some progression path. It's just the same thing over and over and then you die.

I was really enjoying it for a while, though.

Update: OK, they do have difficulty levels. My mistake. I was playing on easy, so I guess I do suck at this game. That doesn't mean I shouldn't be able to enjoy it, though.

developer_response.body:
Hello, 
Thanks for playing, and for your feedback! Glad to hear you're enjoying the core loop. 
We heard the community feedback on the rogue element loud and clear, and we're keeping it in mind for the future. 
Sincerely,
The Ikimasho team
